cases.transcript: All the action. Well, the tool is, the stool, stool is falling over. The boy is taking cookies out of the cookie jar. The little girl is reaching for a cookie and holding her arm up. The mother is drying, is doing dishes. The water is spilling out of the sink. And the mother is standing in the water. Mother is looking out of the window. The water is coming out of the faucet. I don't know if you can get out of that.

cases.transcript: I want you to tell me everything that you see going on in this picture, everything that you see happening here. Go ahead. Well, for one thing, the boy is stealing cookies, and the girl is asking for cookie, and the boy is standing on a stool which is tipping over, and the lady's drying a plate, and the water is running in the sink and overflowing on the floor and getting her feet wet. And the window is open. And I don't know that there's anything special going on outside the window. It seems to me that that's essentially the things that are going on in this picture. And the girl is picking her nose. The boy is handing her a cookie. Really those are the only activities I see. Okay.
cases.patient_only_transcript: Well, for one thing, the boy is stealing cookies, and the girl is asking for cookie, and the boy is standing on a stool which is tipping over, and the lady's drying a plate, and the water is running in the sink and overflowing on the floor and getting her feet wet. And the window is open. And I don't know that there's anything special going on outside the window. It seems to me that that's essentially the things that are going on in this picture. And the girl is picking her nose. The boy is handing her a cookie. Really those are the only activities I see.
speaker_turn_summary: Examiner: I want you to tell me everything that you see going on in this picture, everything that you see happening here. Go ahead.

Patient: Well, for one thing, the boy is stealing cookies, and the girl is asking for cookie, and the boy is standing on a stool which is tipping over, and the lady's drying a plate, and the water is running in the sink and overflowing on the floor and getting her feet wet. And the window is open. And I don't know that there's anything special going on outside the window. It seems to me that that's essentially the things that are going on in this picture. And the girl is picking her nose. The boy is handing her a cookie. Really those are the only activities I see.
Examiner: Okay.

cases.transcript: Hmm, what I want you to do is take a look at this picture and tell me everything that you see happening in this picture. I want you to tell me everything that's going on here. Everything, not things that are just bad, right? Right. Just everything that's going on. Okay. There are two little kids, a boy and a girl. The boy's on a stool which is falling over, getting cookies out of a jar. The cookies are in the cabinet with the lid off, and it's in the kitchen. The mother is washing dishes at the window with a curtain on the window. Walk outside with trees and grass. Water is running over in the sink onto the floor. The mother is drying a dish and stepping in the water. There's a plate and two cups sitting on the cabinet. The mother has an apron on. Okay? Okay.

Water is running over in the sink onto the floor. The mother is drying a dish and stepping in the water. There's a plate and two cups sitting on the cabinet. The mother has an apron on.

cases.transcript: Okay, the sink is running over. She's standing in the sink. Mother is, mother is also drying dishes. Little boy is getting pushed out of the cookie jar, handing it to his sister, and falling off the step stool at the same time. Little girl touching her nose. Outside, you can see the outside window. The cookie jar has fallen over to the side. It looks like the curtains are blowing. And it looks like the mother has completed washing at least two cups and one dish. And action would be the door hanging open where the cookie jar is uncovered.

cases.transcript: Tell me everything that you see going on. Well, a youngster got up on his stool and he's trying to get into the cookie jar and the little girl is already waiting for the cookie, so she's standing there with her hand out. Oh my, and mother is very busy. Oy, she forgot to look at the spigots. They're still open and the water is pouring from the sink and she's looking at it like it's, what am I gonna do now? And there are some dishes on the countertop and you can see out into the yard. The curtains are nice. This little boy is on tilt though. That little stool is going to fall. She has a dish in her hand that she's drying and she looks a little perplexed. Hmm, what am I gonna do with all this water on the floor? And you can see there's another house next door, evidently. Looks like a house and a tree back there beyond the house.
cases.patient_only_transcript: Well, a youngster got up on his stool and he's trying to get into the cookie jar and the little girl is already waiting for the cookie, so she's standing there with her hand out. Oh my, and mother is very busy. Oy, she forgot to look at the spigots. They're still open and the water is pouring from the sink and she's looking at it like it's, what am I gonna do now? And there are some dishes on the countertop and you can see out into the yard. The curtains are nice. This little boy is on tilt though. That little stool is going to fall. cases.transcript: And you can begin now. Kangaroo, horses, dogs, alligators. Oh, does fish? You can. Walruses. Oh, that's another one. Rabbits. Uh-uh, got a block. Got about 30 seconds, as many animals as you can think of. You want no birds or no fish? That's right, okay. Think of that begin with the letter F, and you can begin now. Falling, failure, figure, funny, frigid, fantastic, false, fretful, ferocious, ferocious, frivolous, farm, falcon, false, feather. Did I say father? Okay.

cases.transcript: Think of and you can start now. Cat, dog, mouse, horse, zebra, elephant. When I get to the zoo. Rhinoceros, snake, lion, tiger, jaguar, snake, deer, rabbit, fox. Good grief. Giraffe, rhinoceros, hippopotamus, monkey. Okay, that's the letter F. All the words you can think of that begin with the letter F and you can start now. Farm, fun, feeling. Good grief. Any word at all. Can't even think of F letters. Funny, fin, flower, fox.

cases.transcript: Kid trying to get in the cookie jar and he missed the thing and he's still going out for him and his sister or whoever it is has caught him at it and he's gonna have to do the dishes because his mother can't let him go scot free and of course the water pouring out of the sink onto the floor already she didn't even take the plug out. Okay, anything else? Well, there's other problems here. This isn't the first time this man has done this. He's been doing it all week. Mother doesn't find out until he's big enough to walk. Okay.
cases.patient_only_transcript: Kid trying to get in the cookie jar and he missed the thing and he's still going out for him and his sister or whoever it is has caught him at it and he's gonna have to do the dishes because his mother can't let him go scot free and of course the water pouring out of the sink onto the floor already she didn't even take the plug out. Well, there's other problems here. This isn't the first time this man has done this. He's been doing it all week. Mother doesn't find out until he's big enough to walk.
speaker_turn_summary: Examiner: 'Okay, anything else?'
Patient: Describes cookie jar incident, sink overflow, mother's reaction, and repeated behavior.
Examiner: 'Okay.'
